steps.title: Recognize the population density formula
steps.content: Population density is the total population divided by the total area. We need to divide the number of residents by the area.

steps.title: Calculate the density
steps.content:
Divide 2,556 residents by 142 square miles:
$$\frac{2,556}{142} = 18$$
The population density is 18 residents per square mile. That matches the correct answer, A is correct.
